discovery.run_summary: Tech-led risk-on tape with SMH +4.7% and QQQ +2.2% diverging sharply from flat DIA and weaker XLF/XLV. Movers feed is empty, so no mid-cap discoveries to flag tonight; the actionable signal is the Trump EU tariff deadline post and a memory/AI infrastructure theme building (DRAM ETF inflows, Akamai +20%, Nintendo flagging memory crunch).

notable_index_moves: SMH +4.74% and XLK +3.24% vs DIA flat — narrow leadership, classic semi-led melt-up
AVGO +4.94% and NVDA +2.03% leading; TSM -1.94% diverging negatively, worth watching for tariff/Taiwan-specific concern
XLF -0.57% and KRE -0.39% weak despite risk-on tape — soft underbelly
VIX +1.23% to 17.29 rising alongside SPY up — non-confirmation worth noting

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Memory crunch theme intensifying: Nintendo hiking Switch 2 price $50 citing memory costs, DRAM ETF added $1B in a day, Akamai +20% on $1.8B AI infra deal
TSM -1.94% diverging from semi rally (SMH +4.74%, AVGO +4.94%, NVDA +2.03%)

secondary_opportunities.relationship: TSM customer — divergence suggests tariff/geographic-specific concern at TSM rather than sector

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump set July 4 deadline for EU to ratify trade deal and cut tariffs to zero, threatening 'much higher' tariffs otherwise. Framed as a Von der Leyen call.
trump_signals.note: Standard TACO-pattern setup: hard deadline + threat language, but framed cordially ('great call'). Market has likely learned to fade these — note SPY +0.83% same day. EU autos and luxury most exposed if deadline holds; US importers of EU goods (autos parts, spirits, luxury retail) face cost pressure. Watch for whether EU response is conciliatory or confrontational into next week. Mid-cap US names with EU revenue exposure (e.g., spirits distributors, specialty importers) worth scanning but no specific mid-cap mover to flag tonight.

discovery.no_signals_note: Movers feed was empty — no mid-cap candidates surfaced to analyze. Index/sector data shows a clean semi-led risk-on day with memory as the visible sub-theme, and a Trump EU tariff post that markets shrugged off. Honest output: nothing in the discovery feed to underwrite specific mispricing calls. The catalyst chains and Trump signal above are forward-looking pointers, not reactions to specific moves.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
ai_announcements.summary: OpenAI added voice intelligence features to its API targeting customer service, education, and creator platforms.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: NICE already partners with OpenAI/embeds LLMs; voice API is a building block, not a turnkey contact center. Enterprise CCaaS requires workflow, compliance, WFM that NICE owns.
research_pointers: Check if NICE moved on the news
Compare to prior OpenAI voice releases (Realtime API) and NICE/FIVN reaction

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Five9 has its own AI agents and OpenAI partnership; voice intelligence in API form does not equal a deployable contact center.
research_pointers: Look for sympathy moves vs no market reaction listed

ai_announcements.headline: Perplexity Personal Computer available to all Mac users
ai_announcements.summary: Perplexity's agentic Mac app opens to general availability.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Search query share loss to Perplexity is a slow drip; Google has Gemini agents and Chrome distribution moat. Mac-only agent app is a niche launch.

ai_announcements.headline: SAP acquires Prior Labs for $1B; enterprise AI consolidation
ai_announcements.summary: SAP buys German AI startup Prior Labs; broader theme of enterprise AI M&A heating up.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Acquisition strengthens SAP's Joule/AI roadmap; $1B is small relative to SAP cap and signals offense, not defense.
research_pointers: Confirm Prior Labs deal terms and tech (TabPFN tabular foundation models?)
Read-through to PLTR, ORCL on enterprise AI competitive intensity

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Quiet tape — market_data shows no actual stock moves to react to. News flow is incremental (OpenAI voice API, Perplexity Mac GA, SAP M&A rumor) with no obvious mispriced selloff or under-rally to flag. Anthropic mentioned only in passing (enterprise JV with OpenAI — likely loose reporting); no standalone Anthropic product news requiring bias-flagged call.
